phaseA_interests.text: Help people with personal or emotional problems
Manage a department within a large company
Install software across computers on a large network
Study ways to reduce water pollution
Drive a truck to deliver packages to offices and homes
Examine blood samples using a microscope
Do volunteer work at a non-profit organization
Market a new line of clothing
Test the quality of parts before shipment
Develop a way to better predict the weather
Write scripts for movies or television shows
Sell merchandise at a department store
Stamp, sort, and distribute mail for an organization

phaseB_basic_skills.text: Understanding written sentences and paragraphs in work-related documents.
Giving full attention to what other people are saying and understanding their points.
Using scientific rules and methods to solve problems.
Talking to others to convey information effectively.
Communicating effectively in writing as appropriate for the audience.
Understanding the implications of new information for problem-solving and decision-making.
Using logic and reasoning to evaluate solutions and approaches.
Selecting and using appropriate methods for learning or teaching new things.
Monitoring performance to make improvements or take corrective action.

phaseC_cross_skills.text: Identifying complex problems and developing solutions.
Determining how money will be spent and accounting for expenditures.
Obtaining and managing the use of equipment and materials.
Motivating and directing people as they work.
Managing one's own time and the time of others.
Adjusting actions in relation to others' actions.
Teaching others how to do something.
Bringing others together and reconciling differences.
Persuading others to change their minds or behavior.
Actively looking for ways to help people.
Being aware of others' reactions and understanding them.
Considering costs and benefits to choose appropriate actions.
Determining how a system should work and how changes affect outcomes.
Identifying measures to improve or correct system performance.
Performing routine maintenance on equipment.
Determining the tools and equipment needed for a job.
Installing equipment, machines, or programs to meet specifications.
Controlling operations of equipment or systems.
Analyzing needs and product requirements to create a design.
Monitoring systems to ensure proper operation.
Writing computer programs for various purposes.
Conducting tests and inspections to evaluate quality.
Repairing machines or systems using appropriate tools.
Generating or adapting technology to serve user needs.
Determining causes of errors and deciding what to do.
